headline: 7 Times Oversubscribed. Why Wall Street Is Wild for SK Hynix.
summary: SK Hynix  closed higher Thursday ahead of the South Korean company’s listing of American depositary receipts.  The shares closed up 5.3% in South Korean trading at 2.186 million won, or about $1,445, on Thursday.  After the U.S. close, the company priced its ADR listing at $149 per share, raising $26.5 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal.

After the U.S. close, the company priced its ADR listing at $149 per share, raising $26.5 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal.

headline: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.

content.title: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
content.summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.

The company issued 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.

headline: HBM prices could double by 2027 on surging AI demand, DigiTimes reports
summary: Investing.com -- High-bandwidth memory (HBM) pricing is on track to more than double by 2027, driven by a perfect storm of soaring artificial intelligence demand and structural capacity constraints. According to a DigiTimes report on Friday, industry sources indicate that next-generation HBM4 prices could surge to $4–$5 per gigabit or higher, up from approximately $2 in the second half of 2026. The looming price shock reflects the extreme manufacturing complexities of HBM4, which requires a prod

headline: SK Hynix Stock Spikes in U.S. Trading Debut After Opening at $170
summary: After debuting roughly 14% above the offer price, the South Korean chip maker’s American depositary receipts ticked higher, advancing 17% to $174.45.  The company issued 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.  SK Hynix  priced the ADRs at $149 each on Thursday.

The company issued 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.  SK Hynix  priced the ADRs at $149 each on Thursday.

headline: SK Hynix, Micron, Meta, Delta, Circle Internet, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Stocks were rising slightly Friday and many technology shares were taking a breather as  SK Hynix  made its U.S. trading debut.  American depositary receipts of  SK Hynix   began trading at $170 on the Nasdaq and at last check were at $175.26, up 18% from the initial public offering price of $149.  The South Korean memory chip maker is issuing 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.

headline: Wall Street gets small boost from SK hynix debut
summary: Global stock markets rose slightly on Friday as investors digested a wave of corporate announcements, while the Nasdaq failed to get a major boost from the solid debut of South Korean chip titan SK hynix on Wall Street.SK hynix, along with Samsung and Micron, is a heavyweight in the global market for the high-bandwidth memory used in AI servers alongside other data-crunching semiconductors.

headline: SK Hynix Stock Spikes 13% in U.S. Trading Debut
summary: SK Hynix  shares spiked 13% to $168.49 in their Nasdaq debut as investors scrambled to get a piece of a pivotal player in the global artificial-intelligence supply chain.  The chip maker’s American depositary receipts opened at $170 on Friday and hit a session high of $177, representing a 19% jump from their initial public offering price.  SK Hynix  priced the ADRs at $149 each on Thursday.

headline: How SK Hynix picked the 'perfect time' to list in the US
summary: SK Hynix (SKHYV, 000660.KS) began trading on the Nasdaq after launching its new US listing on Friday. Creative Strategies senior analyst Austin Lyons comes on Market Domination to break down where SK Hynix fits into fulfilling demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) chips.
